title: Editas Medicine Q2 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Editas Medicine Q2 2024 Earnings Summary. Financial Metrics: Cash Position of $318 million in cash and equivalents as of June 30, 2024, down from $377 million in Q1. Burn Rate increased due to higher external research and development expenses, primarily related to clinical and manufacturing costs. Management expects funds to support operations into 2026. Reni-cel Clinical Progress: Clinical data from 18 sickle cell patients (2.4-22.8 months follow-up) and 7 beta thalassemia patients (4.1-12.8 months follow-up) were presented at the EHA 2024 congress. All RUBY trial patients were reported as free from vaso-occlusive events following reni-cel infusion, with mean total hemoglobin levels above 14 grams per deciliter and fetal hemoglobin levels above 40% from six months onward. Manufacturing: Mean neutrophil engraftment of 23 days with low failure rate that continues to improve. Enrollment: Both adult and adolescent cohorts completed for RUBY; adult cohort completed for EdiTHAL. In-Vivo Pipeline Strategy: Editas differentiates itself through functional upregulation using indel CRISPR technology rather than knockdown approaches. Expected to establish in-vivo pre-clinical proof-of-concept for an undisclosed indication by the end of the year. Market Size Threshold: Leadership indicated a strategic ROI threshold of approximately $400-500 million for initial orphan indications. Business Development: Exploring partnerships for reni-cel and monetizing IP portfolio, with executives noting a high double-digits number of programs in development with multiple companies.
